SCOPE DECISION — DOCUMENTED (orchestrator-adjudicated 2026-06-16, option (a): envelope-only forge parity is the correct, COMPLETE forge change for #495):

The forge change is envelope-only BY DESIGN. The boundary-1/boundary-2 framing:
- Root/codex editions have TWO subprocess-spawn boundaries: (boundary-1) claim.js → classifier subprocess [execFileSync on kaola-workflow-classifier.js] — THIS is the #495 bug, fixed by n1's claim.js retry/indeterminate machinery; and (boundary-2) classifier → gh, swallowed to target_unavailable at root kaola-workflow-classifier.js:609-612, which the issue explicitly treats as the GRACEFUL path (out of #495 scope).
- Forge editions have ONLY boundary-2: classifier → glab/tea (forge.viewIssue → CLI exec), swallowed at kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-classifier.js:459-463. This is the analog of root's boundary-2, NOT boundary-1.
- #495's ACs are all about boundary-1 (the claim.js subprocess-spawn catch: spawn-fault/timeout/kill, the classifyIssue spawn helper + retry loop). Forge has NO boundary-1, so the retry/indeterminate heal has nothing to bind to. Envelope-field parity (the result:refuse additions + the indeterminate handler structure, mirrored faithfully into both forge claim.js ports) IS the complete forge change.
- SYMMETRY is the defense: option (a) treats forge IDENTICALLY to root — both leave boundary-2 swallowing, neither retries the internal CLI fetch. Forge is NOT shortchanged.

NOT mirrored (correctly, by design — no forge boundary-1 to bind to): the root classifyIssue() subprocess helpers (classifySubprocessError / classifierTimeoutMs / syncSleepMs), the KAOLA_CLASSIFIER_MOCK_SCRIPT seam, the 3-attempt retry loop, and the indeterminate verdict EMISSION. The indeterminate HANDLERS added to both forge claim.js callers are forward-compat stubs (now annotated inline above each handler): structurally correct envelope parity, currently unreachable because the forge classifier (boundary-2) does not yet emit indeterminate.

RELATED FOLLOW-UP (out of #495 scope): the boundary-2 swallow exists UNIFORMLY across ALL FOUR classifiers (root gh-catch at kaola-workflow-classifier.js:609-612 + forge glab/tea catch at kaola-{gitlab,gitea}-workflow-classifier.js:459-463). Hardening boundary-2 to surface transient CLI-fetch faults (which would then activate these forward-compat handlers) is a real related defect one layer down, edition-uniform, that #495 did not scope — file as a follow-up, not a forge gap.
